mechanism: Security Deny gates are required for every applicable property on every whitelisted resource type. Whether the service default is already compliant is not evaluated.
why: A default is a starting condition, not a control — it can be changed after deployment with no policy signal.

related.note: A compliant default does not substitute for a Deny gate.
A compliant default does not substitute for a Deny gate.
A compliant default does not substitute for a Deny gate.
A compliant default does not substitute for a Deny gate.
When no built-in Deny exists, a custom definition closes the gap — the default does not.

violations: Deny gate omitted because the service default for that property is compliant.
Coverage gap accepted on the basis that the resource type enforces the desired state by default.
